raw_consumer_statement: I went to XXXX XXXX XXXXXX/XX/year>, My bill was {$110.00}. I got charge {$210.00}. I opened the dispute and they gave me partial credit for the difference and then they reversed it They sent me a letter and I had to provide with my receipt. They haven't resolved the issue. I called because I wanted to talk to someone in regards of this because as a customer over 10 years with CITI, I can't believe that even when I had provided with my receipt, they aren't resolving the issue. I asked the agent if he was going ask me the security questions and he said no because it was a transfer call from the XXXX. Then when I started explaining the reason of the call and he started asking the security questions. He asked me for my phone number My security word and then he asked for my full social security which is illegal, they can only ask for the last XXXX and then he denied to help me because I told him that I put it on the system when it prompt me to do which is illegal to denied the service because I didn't provide with the full social security number. At that time, I got upset and let hm know I wanted to talk to a sup or manager and he denied. I told him to transfer the call to cancel my credit card and he denied as well. This is absurd. This is against the law, there are federal regulations that protect the customer to disclose it. This company should get fine for this kind of practices. When I tried to log in into my account online after the conversation and it says that it was temporarily out of service.
